body.title: Inference proxy: apply prompt caching to any supporting provider, not just Anthropic
body.result: cache_control content block on system message for cacheable models in _or_call; 5 tests
body.description:
All providers that support prompt caching should have cache_control applied — currently only Anthropic gets it. As usage-based accounts are replaced by subscription plans, caching reduces cost at every tier.

Rules: if provider response includes cache token fields (any format), apply caching on next request. For OR-routed requests, pass cache_control in the standard Anthropic format where the model supports it (OR forwards it). Add a `supports_caching` flag to ModelSpec so the inference proxy can opt-in per model.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/device.py, devices/inference/models_registry.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first
**Scope boundary:** caching opt-in flag per ModelSpec + inference proxy applies it; no new provider integrations
**Completion criteria:** A cached request to a non-Anthropic model (e.g. via OR) shows cache_read_input_tokens > 0 in the response, OR the request includes cache_control markers and OR confirms support. Test: mock provider response with cache fields → verify flag set.
